summary: The Fed held rates steady at 3.50–3.75% this week, navigating higher-than-expected inflation amid a war. Powell acknowledged a rate hike was discussed, and markets have now pushed the next expected cut to late 2026 at the earliest.
tweet: War and sticky inflation are the twin weights on markets — with PPI hot two months running, the question has shifted from when the Fed cuts to whether it cuts at all.
